widgets.name: Notes
widgets.text:
# AI Agent
Add an gernerative AI agent that can answer questions about how to use the tool. Later it should be trained on SevOne data
# Audit logs link to SevOne audit logs
# Prompts
## Make it start logging prompts to an md file. this is what I have so far.

widgets.name: PM prompt
widgets.text:
You are an experienced project manager. Summarize the following project meeting transcript and create a meeting summary that includes the following items:
•  Meeting Details
o  Meeting Title
o  Project Name (If provided)
o  Date and Time: Clearly state when the meeting took place.
o  Attendees: List all participants with the name of the organization they below to in brackets. For example: participant name (organization name).
•  Meeting Purpose: A brief description of the meeting's objective.
•  Summary of Discussions:
o  Key Topics: Highlight the main subjects discussed.
o  Decisions Made: Outline any decisions or agreements reached.
o  Challenges/Concerns: Note any issues or concerns raised during the meeting.
•  Action Items. Please put the action items into a table format and prioritize them.
o  Task List: Specific actions that need to be taken.
o  Responsible Parties: Assign tasks to individuals or teams.
o  Deadlines: Specify when each action item is due.
•  Next Steps:
o  Follow-Up Meetings: If applicable, mention when the next meeting is scheduled and who will schedule it.
o  Additional Information Needed: Outline any further information required to move forward
•  Key Insights/Takeaways:
o  Summarize the most important points or decisions that are critical for stakeholders to be aware of.
•  Attachments/References:
o  Note any documents or links referenced during the meeting.

Participants

Phill
Ricky
Mohamed

Summary

Naz couldnt attend today and he was driving some of this with his spreadsheet of policies. Mohamed Found some issues with devices that were new, but still not discovering with 0 objects. Some devices on a 151 subnet are failing snmpwalk Commands. A meeting will be scheduled for Naz tomorrow that Mohamed won't be able to join, so we can review VZWAN-124067 (Device Certification ticket). Phill will have action items for the discovery promlem on SevOne-09, and if unable to get it completed in a day, will open a Device Certification case with support (10 business daya SLA).

Concerns

SNMPv3 configurations are using no encryption.
SNMP access is only being configured for 9 IP addresses instead of the 18 meaning failovers would not work.

widgets.name: Big Tasks
widgets.text: # Adding published reports as a web page viewable if not logged in

widgets.name: dfsdf
widgets.text:
You are working on an existing web application that already has:

- A "Reports" page
- A widget system where users can add widgets by choosing:
    - widget type
    - widget name
- An "Integrations" page that already parses SevOne audit logs and displays them in a structured table

Your task is to extend the app with a NEW widget type called:

    "log-parser"

This widget should behave as follows:

--------------------------------------------------
🧩 WIDGET CREATION FLOW
--------------------------------------------------

When the user clicks "Add Widget":

1. The existing widget selector appears
2. The user selects:
       Widget Type = "log-parser"
3. At this moment:
   Instead of immediately adding the widget,
   open a LIGHTBOX / MODAL dialog

--------------------------------------------------
💡 LIGHTBOX / MODAL REQUIREMENTS
--------------------------------------------------

The modal should contain:

1. Widget Name (text input)
   - Pre-filled with the name entered earlier (if available)
   - Required field

2. Log Input (large textarea)
   - Label: "Paste SevOne Audit Log"
   - Accepts multi-line text
   - Required field

3. Buttons:
   - "Create Widget" (primary)
   - "Cancel" (secondary)

--------------------------------------------------
📊 ON SUBMIT BEHAVIOR
--------------------------------------------------

When user clicks "Create Widget":

1. Parse the log text using the SAME logic used on the Integrations page
   (IMPORTANT: reuse existing parsing functions if available)

2. Transform the parsed data into structured rows:
   Each row should include:

   - timestamp
   - userName
   - userId
   - userIp
   - eventType
   - action
   - entityType / relatedEntityType
   - entityName / relatedEntityName
   - success/failure (for auth events)

3. Store the widget config and parsed data:
   Example structure:

   {
     type: "log-parser",
     name: "Widget Name",
     rawLog: "...",
     parsedRows: [...]
   }

--------------------------------------------------
📈 WIDGET DISPLAY REQUIREMENTS
--------------------------------------------------

Render the widget on the Reports page with:

✅ A table that MATCHES the style and behavior of the Integrations page table
   (reuse the same component if possible)

Table should include:
- Column sorting
- Pagination (if already supported)
- Same column structure

--------------------------------------------------
🎨 UI / UX EXPECTATIONS
--------------------------------------------------

- Modal should use existing UI framework styling
- Table must visually match Integrations page
- Widget title should display the widget name
- Widget should be re-renderable from saved state
- Avoid duplicating parsing logic — centralize it

--------------------------------------------------
🔁 BONUS (IF EASY WITH CURRENT ARCHITECTURE)
--------------------------------------------------

- Allow editing the widget (reopen modal with existing data)
- Allow re-parsing when log changes

--------------------------------------------------
⚙️ IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
--------------------------------------------------

- Identify and reuse:
    - existing log parsing utilities
    - existing table component from Integrations page

- Do NOT rewrite parsing logic unless necessary
- Follow current app state management patterns
- Keep code modular (widget component separated from modal)

--------------------------------------------------
✅ OUTPUT EXPECTATION
--------------------------------------------------

Provide:

1. Code changes for:
   - Widget registry / system
   - Modal component
   - Widget component
   - Integration with Reports page

2. Clear explanation of:
   - How parsing is reused
   - How widget data is stored
   - How rendering works

3. Any assumptions you made about the existing app
